_meta.comment: Curated AI risk catalog used by compliance.ComputeRiskRegister to cross-reference detected dependencies against the OWASP ML Top 10 (2023), MITRE ATLAS techniques, and the relevant EU AI Act articles. Keys are matched case-insensitively against AIDependency.Name. This catalog is intentionally small and conservative — it is a static MVP per the 2026-04-26 reassessment and is meant to be extended as we ingest real CVE/GHSA/MITRE feeds in a later wave.

tensorflow.mitigation: Pin tensorflow to an audited version; verify model weight checksums; monitor TensorFlow security advisories at https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/security/advisories.
tensorflow.rationale: TensorFlow has a long history of CVEs in tensor parsing and saved-model deserialisation (CVE-2023-25668, CVE-2022-29216, etc.) that allow remote code execution when loading attacker-controlled models.

torch.mitigation: Use torch.load with weights_only=True (PyTorch >= 1.13); never torch.load() untrusted .pt files. Monitor PyTorch security advisories.
torch.rationale: torch.load() uses pickle internally — loading a malicious weight file executes arbitrary Python in your process.

pytorch.mitigation: Use torch.load with weights_only=True; never torch.load() untrusted .pt files.
pytorch.rationale: torch.load() uses pickle internally — loading a malicious weight file executes arbitrary Python.

transformers.mitigation: Validate and sanitise all text inputs; rate-limit model invocations; log prompts and responses for incident review; consider an LLM firewall (e.g. Lakera Guard, Rebuff).
transformers.rationale: HuggingFace Transformers exposes generative models that are susceptible to prompt-injection and jailbreak attacks at the application boundary.

langchain.mitigation: Disable agents and tool-calling for untrusted inputs; enforce a strict allowlist of tools; require human-in-the-loop approval for destructive operations.
langchain.rationale: LangChain agents that call external tools based on LLM output are a primary prompt-injection attack surface (CVE-2023-29374, CVE-2023-44467).

openai.mitigation: Treat LLM output as untrusted; validate structured outputs against a schema; never execute generated code without sandboxing; rotate API keys; enforce per-user rate limits.
openai.rationale: Direct calls to OpenAI's API expose the application to prompt-injection, output hallucination, and key-exfiltration risks if user input is concatenated into prompts without escaping.

anthropic.mitigation: Treat LLM output as untrusted; validate structured outputs; sandbox any generated code; rotate API keys.
anthropic.rationale: Same prompt-injection / output-trust risks as any third-party LLM API.

huggingface_hub.mitigation: Pin model revisions by SHA, not by tag; verify gated-model licences before automated downloads; mirror critical models internally; scan downloaded weights for executable payloads.
huggingface_hub.rationale: Hugging Face Hub hosts both vetted and arbitrary user-uploaded models; downloading by tag without revision pinning means a poisoned upload can silently replace a trusted model.

scikit-learn.mitigation: Avoid joblib.load() on untrusted .pkl files; prefer ONNX or skops.io.dump for serialisation; track upstream advisories.
scikit-learn.rationale: scikit-learn's recommended pickle/joblib serialisation deserialises arbitrary Python objects — loading attacker-controlled artefacts is RCE-equivalent.

diffusers.mitigation: Pin model revisions by SHA; use safe-tensor format; review NSFW filter status against intended deployment context.
diffusers.rationale: Stable Diffusion and related image-generation pipelines combine the supply-chain risks of huggingface_hub with content-safety obligations under Article 13 (transparency).
